title: Taxonomy + write-time classification enforcement + ranked multi-term search (D7a/b, D8b)
action: Per CONTEXT D7a/b and D8b. RED-FIRST as dp-5. TAXONOMY: docs/decisions/taxonomy.json {schema_version, tags:[{name, description}], candidates:[string]} — hand-curated, read by the CLI when present, written by the CLI only to append unknown-tag candidates (temp-write+rename, never partial). ENFORCEMENT: once taxonomy.json exists, `decisions log` refuses a decide event with zero tags (typed refusal naming --tags); without the file it warns only (bootstrap-safe). Unknown tags (not in tags[] or candidates[]) are ACCEPTED on the event and appended to candidates[] in the same call — never a refusal loop. INHERITANCE (plan-check W3): `decisions supersede` inheritance consults the OVERLAY-APPLIED target — a legacy target classified only via a dp-5 tag event (raw tags undefined) inherits its overlaid tags/scope; with taxonomy present this must NOT produce a false zero-tag refusal (tested). Audit-source events follow the same rule — callers pass tags. SEARCH (plan-check W5): `decisions search --text` upgrades from single substring to multi-term: whitespace-split terms, case-insensitive, OR across terms, matched over decision/rationale/alternatives AND overlaid tags; ranked by deterministic term-hit count descending, then date descending — no wall-clock, byte-stable ordering for a fixed store; single-term results remain a superset of today's substring match; ranking spans the --all archive union (dp-3) with the dp-5 overlay applied. Zero-filter refusal and --tag/--scope/--since AND-composition unchanged. Add `--untagged` filter to search/active listing events with no tags AFTER overlay (the D7d completeness check), composable with --all. Mirror to .bee/bin, update command-registry (both mirrors), render + manifest.

truths: with taxonomy present, an untagged decide log is refused with a typed error; without taxonomy it warns and proceeds
an unknown tag is accepted and lands in taxonomy candidates in the same call
supersede inherits overlaid tags from a retro-tagged raw-untagged target — no false zero-tag refusal
multi-term search ranks by term-hit count then date, deterministically, across the --all union with overlay applied; single-term results include everything today's substring search returns
search matches overlaid tags, not just raw event fields
--untagged lists exactly the events with no tags after overlay, composable with --all
RED-FIRST: failing run against unmodified code recorded as red evidence before implementation
prohibitions: Never refuse an unknown tag (only zero tags, only when taxonomy exists)
Never write taxonomy.json partially — temp-write+rename only
Never let ranking depend on wall-clock or map iteration order
Do not break dp-1 filter semantics or dp-2 supersede inheritance

trace.outcome: Taxonomy + write-time classification enforcement (typed refusal only once taxonomy exists; warn-only bootstrap), unknown tags -> candidates, ranked multi-term OR search over text+tags, --untagged listing
